info.name: Wallet Service
info.description:
Core wallet HTTP API.

**Setup**:
1. Import this collection + `wallet-service.postman_environment.json`.
2. Start the docker stack (`docker compose up -d`) and seed wallets `9701000000000001` + `9701000000000002` (see `wallet_seed.sql`).
3. Run `make run` in `wallet-service/` (binds to `http://localhost:8090` by default).
4. Select the **Wallet Service — local** environment in Postman.
5. Run requests in the order below to exercise the full flow.

**Conventions**:
- Every transactional request auto-generates a unique `reference` in pre-request script (idempotency key).
- `X-Caller-Subject` ↔ `audit.actor` GUC → fills `created_by` / `updated_by` columns.
- `X-Channel` ↔ `audit.channel` GUC → fills `channel` column.
- `X-Request-Id` correlates one client request across services.
- Test scripts capture `tfr_internal_key`, `event_uuid`, `ext_payout_ref` to env vars for downstream requests.

**Error envelope**:
```json
{ "code": "<DOMAIN_CODE>", "message": "...", "request_id": "..." }
```
Codes: `INVALID_REQUEST`, `ACCT_NOT_FOUND`, `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS`, `DR_RESTRAINT_ACTIVE`, `TIER_INSUFFICIENT`, `TIMEOUT`, `WD_NOT_FOUND`, `WD_ALREADY_COMPLETED`, `WD_ALREADY_REVERSED`, ...

exec: // Generate a UNIQUE idempotency reference for every transactional request.
// Each request gets its own value so we never accidentally hit the SP's
// duplicate path with a cached response from a different operation shape.
// (Tests that explicitly verify idempotency override this in their own
// per-request pre-request script — see 'Idempotency' under Error scenarios.)
// Collection-level baseline: every response must carry a request_id header.

request.description: Treasury Service credits a wallet (s2s). 201 POSTED on first call, 200 DUPLICATE on retry with same `reference`.
Wallet → wallet in-book transfer with fee + VAT. Fee config from `WLT_TRAN_DEF.TRFOUT` (5,500 VND fixed, 10% VAT inclusive = 5,000 net + 500 VAT).
Internal transfer with FEE-FREE tran_type (TRFOUTF). Response fee_gross=0, vat_amount=0; no FEETRF leg is posted.

Sync-commit withdraw + insert WLT_WITHDRAW_TRACK (SUBMITTED). Pre-request generates a unique `last_ext_payout_ref` env var that downstream Treasury endpoints reuse.

Fee: 0.1% rate clamped to MIN=11,000 (so withdraw of 200K → 11,000 fee gross, VAT 1,000).

exec: // Generate ext_payout_ref ONCE per run; downstream Treasury endpoints reuse it.

request.description: Merchant settlement withdraw (+ hot-shard sweep when auto_sweep). Requires a seeded merchant group — set {{group_id}} (e.g. run deploy/loadtest/setup.sql or db/tests/wallet_merchant_flow_test.sql). Tolerant of 404/400 when no group is seeded.
Account statement: ledger entries on {{acct_no_a}}, newest-first, keyset-paged (next_cursor → ?before_seq=). Captures a tfr_key for the detail request.
All legs of one transaction by tfr_internal_key (run the list request first to populate {{tfr_key}}).
Reverse an in-book transfer by its ORIGINAL reference (captured by the transfer request into {{last_transfer_reference}}). Run the transfer request first.
Reverse a topup by its ORIGINAL reference (captured into {{last_topup_reference}}). Run the topup request first.
Add a DEBIT hold (locks pledged_amt into available_bal). SP validates type/purpose/conflict/amount/dates. Captures {{restraint_id}} for release.
Release the restraint captured into {{restraint_id}}. reason mandatory for COURT_ORDER/TAX_LIEN.
COURT_ORDER must be type ALL → conflict.
Account profile (no client PII).
Customer realtime balance (§9.3.1). Masked if AML_HOLD restraint active.
Historical end-of-day snapshot from WLT_ACCT_BAL (§9.3.3). today→422 INVALID_DATE; >18m→410 GONE_ONLINE.
Ops/internal full view incl. active_restraints, version, ledger/calc (§9.3.2).
Batch balance, max 100. Missing accts returned with error=ACCT_NOT_FOUND (§9.3.4).

item.name: Treasury Callbacks
item.description:
State machine: SUBMITTED → ACKED → DISBURSING → COMPLETED | FAILED → REVERSED.
Each endpoint targets a specific `ext_payout_ref`; run in order after a withdraw.

request.description: Terminal success state. Idempotent — second call returns the same status without re-emitting outbox.

item.name: POST .../reverse  (auto credit-back on Treasury failure / SLA)

request.description:
Posts RVWD + RVFEE legs, credits the customer wallet back (amount + fee + VAT). Idempotent on `ext_payout_ref`. Refuses to reverse a COMPLETED withdraw — would return 409 WD_ALREADY_COMPLETED.

**Note**: this test will fail with `WD_ALREADY_COMPLETED` if you ran `completed` first. Comment out completed or use a separate withdraw.

item.name: Error scenarios
item.description: Negative tests demonstrating the error envelope + HTTP status mapping.

request.description:
Run TWICE with the SAME `reference` (hard-coded `PM-IDEMPOT-FIXED-001`).

- 1st call → 201 POSTED, balance increases by 100.
- 2nd call → 200 **DUPLICATE**, balance unchanged, same `event_uuid` returned.

Reset by changing the hard-coded reference or wiping the test wallet.

exec: // Override the collection-level auto reference so we ALWAYS use the fixed key
pm.test('POSTED on first run, DUPLICATE on retry',
console.log('Idempotency verified — same tfr_key + event_uuid returned');
